brand.name: SolForge Stocks
brand.tagline: A library for reading stock markets
path.title: Continue with these resources
footer.disclaimer: Educational material for understanding public market information. It does not provide stock recommendations, investment advice or guaranteed returns.
snapshot.title: Major market index snapshot
snapshot.note: SolForge relays public NAVER Finance index responses through its server. Values can be delayed or affected by market closures or provider errors and are not executable prices.
dynamic.partial: Some market data could not be loaded. The explanatory resources below remain available.
dynamic.unavailable: The data provider did not respond, so current market data is unavailable.
koreaDashboard.title: Korean Market Session Dashboard
koreaDashboard.intro: Compare KOSPI and KOSDAQ prices, trading activity, market breadth and participant net purchases on the same trading-day basis.
koreaDashboard.currencyIntro: The won affects foreign flows and each importer's or exporter's revenue and costs differently. Read it with indexes, not as a stand-alone signal.
koreaDashboard.rankIntro: Turnover shows the amount of money participating in a price move, not buying dominance or future returns. The list shows listed stocks and excludes ETFs and ETNs.
koreaDashboard.rankUnavailable: The current turnover ranking is unavailable.
koreaDashboard.note: SolForge normalizes public NAVER Finance responses, and the page refreshes every 60 seconds. Provider, exchange and display times can differ, and the previous session may remain visible on holidays. These are not executable quotes; verify brokerage prices and original KRX and DART disclosures before making a decision.
globalDashboard.title: Global Market Dashboard
globalDashboard.intro: Connect major U.S. and Japanese index prices and session ranges with rates, the dollar, volatility and the most actively traded U.S. stocks on one screen.
globalDashboard.contextIntro: Rates, the dollar and volatility do not move one-for-one with stocks. Compare their direction over the same session with index composition and company results.
globalDashboard.rankTitle: U.S. stocks with the highest turnover
globalDashboard.rankIntro: High turnover identifies where participation is concentrated; it does not imply persistent gains or buying dominance. NASDAQ and NYSE listings are shown separately.
globalDashboard.rankUnavailable: The current U.S. turnover ranking is unavailable.
globalDashboard.note: SolForge normalizes public NAVER Finance responses, and the page refreshes every 60 seconds. Trading hours, currencies and delays differ by market, and the previous session may remain visible on holidays. These are not executable quotes; verify exchange and issuer filings and your broker's live data before making a decision.
pages.home.meta.title: Stock Market Briefing and Investing Basics | SolForge Stocks
pages.home.meta.description: Review major Korean and global indexes, then learn how to read market structure, company material, financial indicators and investment risk.
pages.home.hero.title: Learn an order for reading markets and companies before choosing a stock.
pages.home.hero.body: Review major indexes, then follow a clear sequence through filings, financial statements, valuation and risk. Prepared explanations and verification criteria take priority over search boxes.
pages.home.hero.noteBody: We do not present buy or sell timing for individual stocks. Indexes and financial ratios require a source, reference date and valid comparison group.
pages.home.reading.title: Four layers to separate when reading stock information
pages.home.reading.intro: Market, country, company performance and price answer different questions and should not be compressed into one signal.
pages.home.sections.marketStructure.titl: An index is a reference point for the market, not every stock
pages.home.sections.marketStructure.body: An index follows a defined constituent and weighting method. It can rise while many stocks fall, so compare breadth, sector performance and turnover.
pages.home.sections.korea.title: Korean markets connect flows, currency and disclosures
pages.home.sections.korea.body: Foreign and institutional net purchases are context, not independent signals. Review the won, export-sector weights, exchange filings and earnings dates over the same period.
pages.home.sections.global.title: Global markets use different rates, currencies and sessions
pages.home.sections.global.body: US, Japanese and European indexes trade in different currencies and hours. Separate local-price and KRW returns and treat rates, bond yields and the dollar as distinct variables.
pages.home.sections.company.title: Read business, performance, financial position and price in order
pages.home.sections.company.body: Start with how the company earns money. Review revenue and earnings quality, cash flow, debt and share-count changes before comparing valuation with relevant peers.
pages.koreaMarket.meta.title: How to Read the Korean Stock Market | SolForge Stocks
pages.koreaMarket.meta.description: A Korean stock dashboard for KOSPI and KOSDAQ quotes, volume, turnover, market breadth, participant flows, won exchange rates and turnover leaders.
pages.koreaMarket.hero.title: Read Korean indexes and investor flows in the same context
pages.koreaMarket.hero.body: KOSPI and KOSDAQ have different structures and risks. Start with the session dashboard for indexes, breadth, turnover, participant flows and exchange rates, then connect sector weights and company disclosures.
pages.koreaMarket.hero.noteBody: Index and turnover, market breadth, leading sectors, currency and filings form a useful sequence for separating market-wide and company-specific changes.
pages.koreaMarket.reading.title: Six checks for the Korean market
pages.koreaMarket.reading.intro: Compare values from the same session and venue instead of mixing different aggregation bases.
pages.koreaMarket.sections.kospi.title: KOSPI: check the weight of large companies
pages.koreaMarket.sections.kospi.body: A market-cap-weighted index can be driven by a few large stocks. Compare its change with equal-weighted movement, sector indexes and turnover.
pages.koreaMarket.sections.kosdaq.title: KOSDAQ: pair growth potential with volatility
pages.koreaMarket.sections.kosdaq.body: Its higher growth and small-cap exposure can be sensitive to rates and liquidity. Separate company events such as trials and contracts from financing disclosures.
Participant flows: separate period, market and instrument
Foreign, institutional and retail net purchases are accounting totals with counterparties. Review persistence, spot versus futures and sector distribution rather than one day alone.
pages.koreaMarket.sections.currency.titl: KRW/USD: effects differ by company and investor
pages.koreaMarket.sections.currency.body: A weaker won does not benefit every exporter equally. Check sales currency, imported inputs, hedging, foreign-currency debt and foreign investor flows.
pages.koreaMarket.sections.sectors.title: Sector weights explain gaps between indexes and experience
pages.koreaMarket.sections.sectors.body: Semiconductors, autos or biotech can drive an index. Companies inside one sector still have different customers and value-chain positions, so common movement can have different causes.
pages.koreaMarket.sections.disclosures.t: Filings and schedules: read originals and corrections
pages.koreaMarket.sections.disclosures.b: Earnings, offerings, convertible securities, ownership changes and suspensions require the original filing. Check submission date, covered period, amendments and attachments.
pages.globalMarket.meta.title: How to Read Global Stock Markets | SolForge Stocks
pages.globalMarket.meta.description: Review major U.S. and Japanese indexes, Treasury yields, the dollar, VIX and turnover leaders, then learn how to read global markets.
pages.globalMarket.hero.title: Read overseas indexes with rates, currencies and market hours
pages.globalMarket.hero.body: Use the dashboard below to review major U.S. and Japanese indexes, rates, the dollar, volatility and turnover leaders. The same company news can be priced differently across currencies and regular or extended-hours sessions.
pages.globalMarket.hero.noteBody: Local share returns and KRW returns differ. Review commissions, conversion costs, dividend withholding and available trading hours separately.
pages.globalMarket.reading.title: Six bases for comparing global markets
pages.globalMarket.reading.intro: National indexes are not interchangeable products; distinguish sectors, currency and trading systems.
pages.globalMarket.sections.indexes.titl: Index composition: S&P 500 and NASDAQ ask different questions
pages.globalMarket.sections.indexes.body: The S&P 500 broadly represents large US companies, while the NASDAQ Composite covers Nasdaq listings. Check technology exposure and weighting before comparison.
pages.globalMarket.sections.rates.title: Rates affect discount rates and financing costs
pages.globalMarket.sections.rates.body: Policy and government-bond rates affect growth valuations, bank profitability and borrowing through different channels. Review the reason for a rate move and its maturity.
pages.globalMarket.sections.dollar.title: The dollar affects converted returns and company results
pages.globalMarket.sections.dollar.body: A stronger dollar can improve a Korean investor's converted return while pressuring translation for US multinationals. Separate investor and company exposure.
pages.globalMarket.sections.earnings.tit: Earnings: separate reported results from guidance
pages.globalMarket.sections.earnings.bod: Review revenue and earnings versus expectations along with guidance, margins, backlog and one-offs. Thin after-hours prices can differ from the next regular session.
pages.globalMarket.sections.sectors.titl: Sector rotation divides performance inside one index
pages.globalMarket.sections.sectors.body: Technology, finance, energy and healthcare respond differently to rates and growth. Sector ETFs are context and do not replace company-level analysis.
pages.globalMarket.sections.sessions.tit: Sessions: verify date, time zone and holidays
pages.globalMarket.sections.sessions.bod: Korean dates can differ from local trading dates, and daylight saving changes opening times. Do not mix previous close, premarket and after-hours quotes.
pages.indicators.meta.title: How to Read Stock Financial Indicators | SolForge Stocks
pages.indicators.meta.description: The meaning and limits of price, volume, market cap, P/E, P/B, profitability and financial-stability measures.
pages.indicators.hero.title: Check the formula and comparison group before relying on one ratio
pages.indicators.hero.body: P/E, P/B and ROE compress one aspect of a company. Meaning depends on numerator, denominator, period, one-offs and industry differences.
pages.indicators.hero.noteBody: Identify trailing or forward estimates, consolidated or separate accounts, and consistent currency and accounting standards before peer comparison.
pages.indicators.reading.title: The question and limit behind each indicator
pages.indicators.reading.intro: A high or low number does not by itself mean expensive or cheap.
pages.indicators.sections.price.title: Price and return: verify date and adjustments
pages.indicators.sections.price.body: Adjusted prices can differ from actual closes after dividends and splits. Use the same adjustment and currency basis for period returns.
pages.indicators.sections.volume.title: Volume: observe participation behind a price move
pages.indicators.sections.volume.body: Compare volume and turnover with averages. Check whether block trades, index rebalancing or expiration created a temporary spike.
pages.indicators.sections.marketCap.titl: Market cap is the market's equity valuation
pages.indicators.sections.marketCap.body: Price multiplied by shares outstanding does not directly subtract cash or debt. Enterprise-value comparisons can require another basis.
pages.indicators.sections.valuation.titl: P/E and P/B compare price with earnings and equity
pages.indicators.sections.valuation.body: P/E is not meaningful for losses, and suitable ranges differ across banks, platforms and manufacturers. Review normalized earnings, asset quality and expected growth.
ROE and margins test the persistence of returns
High ROE can result from debt or a small equity base. Compare operating margin, asset turnover and multi-year change.
pages.indicators.sections.stability.titl: Debt and cash flow test resilience
pages.indicators.sections.stability.body: A debt ratio does not reveal maturity or interest rates. Review interest coverage, cash, operating cash flow and future investment and repayment needs.
pages.companyReading.meta.title: An Order for Reading Companies and Financial Statements | SolForge Stocks
pages.companyReading.meta.description: A sequence for reviewing business model, revenue, earnings, balance sheet, cash flow and governance.
pages.companyReading.hero.title: Start with the business and follow the numbers through to cash
pages.companyReading.hero.body: Read business descriptions, customer and product mix and financial statements before news and price. Ask whether accounting earnings become cash.
pages.companyReading.hero.noteBody: Investor presentations are useful but selective. Recheck definitions in audited statements, exchange filings and notes.
pages.companyReading.reading.title: Six stages for reading a company
pages.companyReading.reading.intro: Separate business quality, accounting numbers and market price before connecting them.
pages.companyReading.sections.business.t: 1. What does it sell and to whom?
pages.companyReading.sections.business.b: Identify products, customers, regions, competitive advantages and regulation. Customer or product concentration can increase growth and risk together.
pages.companyReading.sections.revenue.ti: 2. Where did revenue growth come from?
pages.companyReading.sections.revenue.bo: Separate volume, price, currency and acquisition effects. Check whether one-off contracts or consolidation changes appear as repeatable growth.
pages.companyReading.sections.earnings.t: 3. What is the quality of earnings?
pages.companyReading.sections.earnings.b: Review operating versus net income, disposal gains, subsidies and valuation changes. Explain margin changes that outpace revenue.
pages.companyReading.sections.balance.ti: 4. What is the quality of assets and liabilities?
pages.companyReading.sections.balance.bo: Review inventory, receivables, debt maturity, intangibles and provisions in the notes. Book value does not guarantee cash recovery.
pages.companyReading.sections.cashflow.b: Compare operating cash, capital expenditure and free cash flow over several years. Identify temporary help from working capital or asset sales.
pages.companyReading.sections.governance: 6. How do share count and incentives change?
Offerings, convertibles, options and treasury-share treatment affect per-share value. Review controlling-owner and related-party transactions.
pages.risk.meta.title: Understanding Stock Investment Risk | SolForge Stocks
pages.risk.meta.description: Practical checks for volatility, concentration, currency, liquidity, leverage and event risk.
pages.risk.hero.title: Understand loss paths before estimating returns
pages.risk.hero.body: A strong company can still produce a loss through excessive price, low liquidity, currency moves or unexpected filings. Separate business risk from position risk.
pages.risk.hero.noteBody: Separate living expenses from risk capital and avoid leverage or single-stock concentration that can create unaffordable losses.
pages.risk.reading.title: Six risks and how to check them
pages.risk.reading.intro: Review company, market and account levels because several risks can occur together.
pages.risk.sections.volatility.title: Price volatility
pages.risk.sections.volatility.body: Earnings and news can trigger sharp moves or trading halts. Historical volatility does not cap future loss.
pages.risk.sections.concentration.title: Stock and sector concentration
pages.risk.sections.concentration.body: Several holdings can still share exposure to one industry, country or rate factor. Map overlapping revenue and risk drivers.
pages.risk.sections.currency.title: Currency risk
pages.risk.sections.currency.body: A foreign share can rise while KRW appreciation reduces converted return. Calculate company currency exposure and investor conversion exposure separately.
pages.risk.sections.liquidity.title: Liquidity and execution risk
pages.risk.sections.liquidity.body: During thin or stressed trading, a displayed price may not be available for your order size. Review spreads, turnover and market-order risk.
pages.risk.sections.leverage.title: Margin, credit and derivatives
pages.risk.sections.leverage.body: Borrowing magnifies small moves and forced-sale risk. Avoid structures whose interest, margin, maturity and worst loss you do not understand.
pages.risk.sections.events.title: Filings, policy and trading interruption
pages.risk.sections.events.body: Accounting revisions, regulation, litigation, delisting procedures and exchange failures can limit reaction. Review official rules and account terms.
pages.methodology.meta.title: Stock Data Sources and Editorial Policy | SolForge Stocks
pages.methodology.meta.description: How SolForge Stocks handles public data, refresh timing, delays, adjusted prices, missing fields and editorial independence.
pages.methodology.hero.title: We disclose source, reference time and limits
pages.methodology.hero.body: The Korean and global market dashboards load public market responses through the same-site API, while guides follow market-structure and financial-statement review sequences.
pages.methodology.hero.noteBody: Before trading, verify your exchange's live order book, official company filings and current regulator and tax guidance.
pages.methodology.reading.title: How we handle data and explanations
pages.methodology.reading.intro: Missing values remain unavailable rather than being filled with estimates.
pages.methodology.sections.sources.title: Public data sources
pages.methodology.sections.sources.body: Korean and global indexes, Korean market breadth and participant flows, exchange rates, U.S. Treasury yields, the Dollar Index, VIX and turnover leaders normalize public NAVER Finance responses through the SolForge server. Company review guidance prioritizes original exchange and issuer filings.
pages.methodology.sections.refresh.title: Refresh timing
pages.methodology.sections.refresh.body: A recent response is requested when the page opens, and the Korean and global market dashboards refresh every 60 seconds while visible. Display check time, exchange execution time and provider update time can differ.
pages.methodology.sections.delay.title: Quote delays and closures
pages.methodology.sections.delay.body: Delay varies by market and instrument, and a prior close can remain on holidays. Values are not executable prices or trading signals.
pages.methodology.sections.adjustment.bo: Charts differ depending on treatment of splits, dividends and rights. Check the provider's adjustment basis before comparing long returns.
pages.methodology.sections.missing.title: Missing and failed requests
pages.methodology.sections.missing.body: External failures or missing fields leave the current value unavailable. Static explanations remain readable during data outages.
pages.methodology.sections.disclaimer.bo: We provide no stock ranking or target price and correct errors after source review. This is not individualized investment, legal or tax advice.
pages.about.meta.title: About | SolForge Stocks
pages.about.meta.description: The mission, coverage, bilingual operation and editorial independence of SolForge Stocks.
pages.about.hero.title: An independent library for reading markets and company material
pages.about.hero.body: Prepared explanations that teach readers how to use public market and company information take priority over ticker-input tools.
pages.about.hero.noteBody: We connect market structure, financial statements, risk and data limits instead of repeating short stock opinions.
pages.about.reading.title: What the site provides
pages.about.reading.intro: Korean and global markets, indicators, company reading and risk share one navigable structure.
pages.about.sections.mission.title: Mission
pages.about.sections.mission.body: Provide sources and comparison criteria so readers can judge data rather than see price figures alone.
pages.about.sections.scope.title: Coverage
pages.about.sections.scope.body: Major indexes, market context, financial measures, filings, financial statements and general investment risk.
pages.about.sections.bilingual.title: Korean and English
pages.about.sections.bilingual.body: Core pages share the same structure in both languages, and the language switch preserves the current path.
pages.about.sections.independence.title: Editorial independence
pages.about.sections.independence.body: No broker or stock currently sponsors rankings. Future advertising or affiliations will be separated and disclosed.
pages.privacy.meta.title: Privacy | SolForge Stocks
pages.privacy.meta.description: How SolForge Stocks handles user input, quote APIs, hosting logs and potential advertising technologies.
pages.privacy.hero.title: Minimize collection and disclose external data connections
pages.privacy.hero.body: The independent stock site currently requires no account or portfolio input. External public APIs support the index snapshot.
pages.privacy.hero.noteBody: Never enter a brokerage account number, password, authentication code or government identifier here. SolForge does not request them.
pages.privacy.reading.title: Current processing scope
pages.privacy.reading.intro: This policy will be updated to match actual behavior if features or advertising change.
pages.privacy.sections.inputs.title: User input
pages.privacy.sections.inputs.body: The site does not currently collect or store accounts, portfolios, contact details or payment information on a server.
pages.privacy.sections.apis.title: External quote APIs
pages.privacy.sections.apis.body: The browser calls only the same-site SolForge API for indexes, exchange rates and market dashboard data. Cloudflare may process connection data, while external market-data requests are made by the server.
pages.privacy.sections.hosting.title: Hosting and access logs
pages.privacy.sections.hosting.body: Cloudflare Pages delivers content and may process basic logs for security, reliability and statistics under Cloudflare policy.
pages.privacy.sections.advertising.title: Advertising and cookies
pages.privacy.sections.advertising.body: No advertising script is included at launch. Future ads will not cover main content and will include required notices and consent.
pages.privacy.sections.contact.title: Contact and changes
pages.privacy.sections.contact.body: Privacy questions can be submitted through the main SolForge contact page. Processing changes will also update this page.
